author: y4rr
content:
Plan: ConvNeXt-T's first 7×7 conv per block is currently a regular 7×7 Conv2d (see ferrotorch-vision/src/models/convnext.rs:5-7 'regular 7x7 convolution because grouped/depthwise convolutions are not yet available in ferrotorch_nn. This changes the parameter count'). Torchvision's ConvNeXt-T uses depthwise 7×7 (groups=C). Until ferrotorch_nn gains grouped/depthwise Conv2d the named_parameters() schema cannot match torchvision convnext_tiny state_dict keys.

Phase 3 (#996) migrated ConvNeXt's permute-pattern CPU-pull bug (the channel_layer_norm helpers nhwc_from_nchw / nchw_from_nhwc) onto Tensor::permute(...).contiguous(). The CPU-pull bug is fixed. The architectural divergence (depthwise) remains and is escalated here.

Acceptance: ferrotorch-nn gains grouped/depthwise Conv2d primitive; convnext.rs swaps the regular 7×7 for depthwise; convnext_tiny_value_parity in conformance_vision_models.rs drops the #[ignore] and passes torch.allclose against tvm.convnext_tiny(weights=None).

Out of scope here: any change to the Phase 3 permute migration.
